meta.name: PT2 Domain Ontology
meta.description: Structured domain ontology for PyTorch 2 (torch.compile), mined from 6,167 GitHub issues (3,876 closed, 2,291 open)

scopes.torch_compile: Core torch.compile stack (Dynamo, Inductor, holistic areas) — current focus
scopes.shared: Cross-cutting entities used by all sub-ontologies

visibility.oss: Open-source — exists in public PyTorch repo and GitHub issues
visibility.internal: Meta-internal only — not in public repo (e.g., MegaCache, internal backends, Meta-specific configs)

meta.changelog: v0.9.0: Full dataset download (6,167 issues, 4,677 comments), Phase 1 diagnostic extraction (2,606 user issues), 15 user-fix shortcuts, diagnostic decision tree (9 symptom entry points), data source comparison (Hive vs GitHub API); v0.8.0: +23 label mappings (95.2% classification), +8 causes, +9 symptoms, 228 resolved_by PR edges, 107 weighted edges, expert mapping (27 entries), visibility field for OSS vs internal distinction; v0.7.0: STO owners and GitHub label mappings on 43 components (from PT2 STO Structure + Issue Health Design Doc); 4 new components (PT2-Dispatch, vLLM Compile, Compiled Autograd, Helion); v0.6.1: Platform entity type — hardware accelerators (NVIDIA GPU, AMD GPU, Intel XPU, TPU, ARM CPU, Apple MPS) and OS (Linux, Windows, macOS) with default/non-default classification; v0.6.0: Beaver cause tree merge — 89 leaf subcauses from 9,277 GitHub issues with evidence counts and example issue IDs; v0.5.1: is_subcause_of tree, fix_type taxonomy, resolved_by, diagnosed_by enrichment; v0.5.0: user journeys, 6 new relationship types; v0.4.0: Beaver entity extraction

entity_types.component: A software component or subsystem in the PT2 stack
entity_types.failure_mode: A category of things that can go wrong
entity_types.symptom: An observable signal that something is wrong
entity_types.cause: A root cause that triggers a failure mode
entity_types.resolution: A type of fix or workaround
entity_types.model: A user model or model family affected by issues
entity_types.ecosystem: An ecosystem project, benchmark, or framework that interacts with PT2
entity_types.optimization: A specific optimization technique or pass
entity_types.user_journey: A user-facing problem scenario that defines a diagnostic and resolution workflow
entity_types.platform: A hardware accelerator or operating system that affects issue diagnosis and support level

relationship_types.is_component_of: X is a sub-component of Y
relationship_types.depends_on: X depends on Y at runtime or compile time
relationship_types.fixed_by: X (failure_mode) can be resolved by Y (resolution)
relationship_types.diagnosed_by: X (failure_mode/journey) is diagnosed using Y (diagnostic tool/config)
relationship_types.resolved_by: X (failure_mode/journey) is resolved by Y (resolution), with fix_type property
relationship_types.routes_to: X (journey) routes to Y (journey) when diagnostic reveals specific root cause
relationship_types.enters_via: X (journey) is entered when user observes Y (symptom/failure_mode)
relationship_types.involves: X (journey) involves Y (component/config/op) as relevant context
relationship_types.is_subcause_of: X (specific cause) is a sub-cause of Y (cause category) — supports funnel/tree structure
relationship_types.platform_specific: X (cause/failure_mode) is specific to or primarily affects Y (platform)
